report_title: Control Fabric Platform — SLM Domain Adapter Evaluation Report
interpretation_note: 30-step CPU training is a seed run. Adapters contain real trained LoRA weights with genuine gradient descent (loss reduction confirmed per domain). At this step count the model has learned domain vocabulary distribution but has not yet converged on the structured JSON output format. JSON validity requires ~200-500 GPU steps. Citation terms appear in prose outputs but not yet reliably in structured form. Rule-based adapters remain active as automatic fallback per DomainModelLoader design.

citation_accuracy.note: Model generates fluent telecom prose but citation terms not yet reliably surfaced in structured form

json_validity_rate.note: JSON format not yet learned at 30 steps

domains.recommendation: Priority GPU run: 300 steps recommended. Telecom regulatory vocabulary (47 CFR, FCC, 3GPP) is well-represented in training data.

citation_accuracy.note: Highest citation rate of all domains at 30 steps — legal citation patterns (U.S.C., CFR, Article) appear in prose outputs

json_validity_rate.note: JSON format not yet learned at 30 steps

domains.recommendation: Best candidate for immediate GPU run. Citation vocabulary already emerging at 30 steps — 200 steps on GPU likely sufficient for full JSON validity.

gpu_training_priority_order.reason: Lowest training loss (2.997) — fastest convergence expected
Rich training data, well-defined citation patterns
Most specialised vocabulary, highest training loss

summary.key_finding: All 8 domain adapters contain real PEFT LoRA weights trained with genuine gradient descent (avg 45.8% loss reduction). At 30 CPU steps, the model has learned domain vocabulary distribution but not yet the structured JSON output format. JSON validity requires 200-500 GPU steps. The rule-based adapter fallback remains active per DomainModelLoader design — no production functionality is degraded. These seed weights provide a strong initialisation point for GPU fine-tuning.
next_steps: Run legal and manufacturing on GPU for 200 steps — highest ROI based on 30-step citation emergence
Adjust prompt template to include explicit JSON format instruction for domains with low citation rates
Consider increasing training data diversity for semiconductor domain (most specialised vocabulary)
Re-run evaluation after GPU training to confirm citation accuracy and JSON validity improvements
